authors_text: Mark Pritt
title: Deep Learning for Recognizing Mobile Targets in Satellite Imagery
title_zh: 用于识别卫星图像中移动目标的深度学习
comment: 7 pages, 15 figures, 2018 IEEE Applied Imagery Pattern Recognition Workshop (AIPR)
comments: 7 pages, 15 figures, 2018 IEEE Applied Imagery Pattern Recognition Workshop (AIPR)
abstract: There is an increasing demand for software that automatically detects and classifies mobile targets such as airplanes, cars, and ships in satellite imagery. Applications of such automated target recognition (ATR) software include economic forecasting, traffic planning, maritime law enforcement, and disaster response. This paper describes the extension of a convolutional neural network (CNN) for classification to a sliding window algorithm for detection. It is evaluated on mobile targets of the xView dataset, on which it achieves detection and classification accuracies higher than 95%.
summary: There is an increasing demand for software that automatically detects and classifies mobile targets such as airplanes, cars, and ships in satellite imagery. Applications of such automated target recognition (ATR) software include economic forecasting, traffic planning, maritime law enforcement, and disaster response. This paper describes the extension of a convolutional neural network (CNN) for classification to a sliding window algorithm for detection. It is evaluated on mobile targets of the xView dataset, on which it achieves detection and classification accuracies higher than 95%.
abstract_zh: 人们对自动检测和分类卫星图像中的飞机、汽车和船舶等移动目标的软件的需求不断增长。这种自动目标识别（ATR）软件的应用包括经济预测、交通规划、海上执法和灾难响应。本文描述了用于分类的卷积神经网络（CNN）到用于检测的滑动窗口算法的扩展。它在 xView 数据集的移动目标上进行了评估，检测和分类准确率高于 95%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习的卫星图像移动目标检测与分类方法。
AI.motivation: 自动检测卫星图像中的飞机、汽车、船只等移动目标在经济预测、交通规划等领域有重要应用需求。
AI.method: 将卷积神经网络（CNN）分类模型扩展为滑动窗口检测算法，用于移动目标识别。
AI.result: 在xView数据集上实现检测和分类准确率均超过95%。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习方法有效提升了卫星图像中移动目标识别的性能，验证了该技术的可行性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习的卫星图像移动目标检测与分类方法。
ai_content.motivation: 自动检测卫星图像中的飞机、汽车、船只等移动目标在经济预测、交通规划等领域有重要应用需求。
ai_content.method: 将卷积神经网络（CNN）分类模型扩展为滑动窗口检测算法，用于移动目标识别。
ai_content.result: 在xView数据集上实现检测和分类准确率均超过95%。
ai_content.conclusion: 深度学习方法有效提升了卫星图像中移动目标识别的性能，验证了该技术的可行性。

search_text: Deep Learning for Recognizing Mobile Targets in Satellite Imagery 用于识别卫星图像中移动目标的深度学习 There is an increasing demand for software that automatically detects and classifies mobile targets such as airplanes, cars, and ships in satellite imagery. Applications of such automated target recognition (ATR) software include economic forecasting, traffic planning, maritime law enforcement, and disaster response. This paper describes the extension of a convolutional neural network (CNN) for classification to a sliding window algorithm for detection. It is evaluated on mobile targets of the xView dataset, on which it achieves detection and classification accuracies higher than 95%. 人们对自动检测和分类卫星图像中的飞机、汽车和船舶等移动目标的软件的需求不断增长。这种自动目标识别（ATR）软件的应用包括经济预测、交通规划、海上执法和灾难响应。本文描述了用于分类的卷积神经网络（CNN）到用于检测的滑动窗口算法的扩展。它在 xView 数据集的移动目标上进行了评估，检测和分类准确率高于 95%。 本文提出一种基于深度学习的卫星图像移动目标检测与分类方法。 Mark Pritt cs.CV

authors_text: Maria Attarian,Brett D. Roads,Michael C. Mozer
title: Transforming Neural Network Visual Representations to Predict Human Judgments of Similarity
title_zh: 转换神经网络视觉表示以预测人类对相似性的判断
abstract: Deep-learning vision models have shown intriguing similarities and differences with respect to human vision. We investigate how to bring machine visual representations into better alignment with human representations. Human representations are often inferred from behavioral evidence such as the selection of an image most similar to a query image. We find that with appropriate linear transformations of deep embeddings, we can improve prediction of human binary choice on a data set of bird images from 72% at baseline to 89%. We hypothesized that deep embeddings have redundant, high (4096) dimensional representations; however, reducing the rank of these representations results in a loss of explanatory power. We hypothesized that the dilation transformation of representations explored in past research is too restrictive, and indeed we found that model explanatory power can be significantly improved with a more expressive linear transform. Most surprising and exciting, we found that, consistent with classic psychological literature, human similarity judgments are asymmetric: the similarity of X to Y is not necessarily equal to the similarity of Y to X, and allowing models to express this asymmetry improves explanatory power.
summary: Deep-learning vision models have shown intriguing similarities and differences with respect to human vision. We investigate how to bring machine visual representations into better alignment with human representations. Human representations are often inferred from behavioral evidence such as the selection of an image most similar to a query image. We find that with appropriate linear transformations of deep embeddings, we can improve prediction of human binary choice on a data set of bird images from 72% at baseline to 89%. We hypothesized that deep embeddings have redundant, high (4096) dimensional representations; however, reducing the rank of these representations results in a loss of explanatory power. We hypothesized that the dilation transformation of representations explored in past research is too restrictive, and indeed we found that model explanatory power can be significantly improved with a more expressive linear transform. Most surprising and exciting, we found that, consistent with classic psychological literature, human similarity judgments are asymmetric: the similarity of X to Y is not necessarily equal to the similarity of Y to X, and allowing models to express this asymmetry improves explanatory power.
abstract_zh: 深度学习视觉模型与人类视觉表现出有趣的相似点和差异。我们研究如何使机器视觉表征与人类表征更好地保持一致。人类表征通常是从行为证据中推断出来的，例如选择与查询图像最相似的图像。我们发现，通过对深度嵌入进行适当的线性变换，我们可以将人类对鸟类图像数据集的二元选择的预测从基线的 72% 提高到 89%。我们假设深度嵌入具有冗余的高维表示（4096）；然而，降低这些表示的等级会导致解释力的丧失。我们假设过去研究中探索的表示的膨胀变换过于严格，事实上我们发现通过更具表现力的线性变换可以显着提高模型的解释能力。最令人惊讶和兴奋的是，我们发现，与经典心理学文献一致，人类的相似性判断是不对称的：X与Y的相似性不一定等于Y与X的相似性，并且允许模型表达这种不对称性可以提高解释力。

AI.tldr: 该论文研究如何通过线性变换对齐神经网络的视觉表示与人类判断，以提高预测人类相似性选择的准确性。
AI.motivation: 深度学习视觉模型与人类视觉存在差异，需改进机器表示以更贴近人类认知，从而提升模型解释性与实用性。
AI.method: 通过对深度嵌入进行线性变换（如降维和不对称性建模），优化预测人类二元选择的准确性，并验证冗余维度与不对称性的影响。
AI.result: 在鸟类图像数据集上，模型预测准确率从基线72%提升至89%，发现人类相似性判断具有不对称性且引入该特性显著提升模型表现。
AI.conclusion: 线性变换能有效对齐机器与人类视觉表示，而捕捉人类相似性判断的不对称性是提升模型解释力的关键。

authors_text: Behzad Ghazanfari,Fatemeh Afghah,Sixian Zhang
title: Piece-wise Matching Layer in Representation Learning for ECG Classification
title_zh: 心电图分类表示学习中的分段匹配层
abstract: This paper proposes piece-wise matching layer as a novel layer in representation learning methods for electrocardiogram (ECG) classification. Despite the remarkable performance of representation learning methods in the analysis of time series, there are still several challenges associated with these methods ranging from the complex structures of methods, the lack of generality of solutions, the need for expert knowledge, and large-scale training datasets. We introduce the piece-wise matching layer that works based on two levels to address some of the aforementioned challenges. At the first level, a set of morphological, statistical, and frequency features and comparative forms of them are computed based on each periodic part and its neighbors. At the second level, these features are modified by predefined transformation functions based on a receptive field scenario. Several scenarios of offline processing, incremental processing, fixed sliding receptive field, and event-based triggering receptive field can be implemented based on the choice of length and mechanism of indicating the receptive field. We propose dynamic time wrapping as a mechanism that indicates a receptive field based on event triggering tactics. To evaluate the performance of this method in time series analysis, we applied the proposed layer in two publicly available datasets of PhysioNet competitions in 2015 and 2017 where the input data is ECG signal. We compared the performance of our method against a variety of known tuned methods from expert knowledge, machine learning, deep learning methods, and the combination of them. The proposed approach improves the state of the art in two known completions 2015 and 2017 around 4% and 7% correspondingly while it does not rely on in advance knowledge of the classes or the possible places of arrhythmia.
summary: This paper proposes piece-wise matching layer as a novel layer in representation learning methods for electrocardiogram (ECG) classification. Despite the remarkable performance of representation learning methods in the analysis of time series, there are still several challenges associated with these methods ranging from the complex structures of methods, the lack of generality of solutions, the need for expert knowledge, and large-scale training datasets. We introduce the piece-wise matching layer that works based on two levels to address some of the aforementioned challenges. At the first level, a set of morphological, statistical, and frequency features and comparative forms of them are computed based on each periodic part and its neighbors. At the second level, these features are modified by predefined transformation functions based on a receptive field scenario. Several scenarios of offline processing, incremental processing, fixed sliding receptive field, and event-based triggering receptive field can be implemented based on the choice of length and mechanism of indicating the receptive field. We propose dynamic time wrapping as a mechanism that indicates a receptive field based on event triggering tactics. To evaluate the performance of this method in time series analysis, we applied the proposed layer in two publicly available datasets of PhysioNet competitions in 2015 and 2017 where the input data is ECG signal. We compared the performance of our method against a variety of known tuned methods from expert knowledge, machine learning, deep learning methods, and the combination of them. The proposed approach improves the state of the art in two known completions 2015 and 2017 around 4% and 7% correspondingly while it does not rely on in advance knowledge of the classes or the possible places of arrhythmia.
abstract_zh: 本文提出分段匹配层作为心电图（ECG）分类表示学习方法中的一个新颖层。尽管表示学习方法在时间序列分析中表现出色，但这些方法仍然面临一些挑战，包括方法结构复杂、解决方案缺乏通用性、需要专家知识和大规模训练数据集。我们引入了基于两个级别的分段匹配层来解决上述一些挑战。在第一级，根据每个周期部分及其邻居计算一组形态、统计和频率特征及其比较形式。在第二级，这些特征通过基于感受野场景的预定义转换函数进行修改。根据感受野长度和指示机制的选择，可以实现离线处理、增量处理、固定滑动感受野、基于事件触发感受野等几种场景。我们提出动态时间包裹作为一种机制，指示基于事件触发策略的感受野。为了评估该方法在时间序列分析中的性能，我们在 2015 年和 2017 年 PhysioNet 竞赛的两个公开数据集中应用了所提出的层，其中输入数据是心电图信号。我们将我们的方法的性能与来自专家知识、机器学习、深度学习方法及其组合的各种已知调整方法进行了比较。所提出的方法将 2015 年和 2017 年两个已知完成的现有技术水平相应提高了约 4% 和 7%，同时它不依赖于对心律失常的类别或可能位置的预先了解。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种分段匹配层，用于心电图（ECG）分类的表示学习方法，以解决现有方法在复杂结构、通用性不足、依赖专家知识和大规模数据集等方面的挑战。
AI.motivation: 尽管表示学习方法在时间序列分析中表现优异，但其复杂结构、缺乏通用性、依赖专家知识及需要大规模训练数据等问题仍需改进。本文旨在通过分段匹配层提升ECG分类的性能，同时减少对先验知识的依赖。
AI.method: 分段匹配层通过两级处理实现：第一级计算周期性部分及其邻居的形态学、统计学和频率特征；第二级基于感受野场景通过预定义变换函数调整特征。支持离线处理、增量处理、固定滑动窗口和事件触发等多种场景，并引入动态时间包装机制定义感受野。
AI.result: 在PhysioNet 2015和2017公开数据集上，该方法分别较现有方法提升约4%和7%，且无需提前知晓类别或心律失常可能位置。
AI.conclusion: 分段匹配层通过双级特征处理和灵活的感受野机制，在ECG分类任务中显著提升性能，同时降低对专家知识和大规模数据的依赖，为时间序列分析提供有效解决方案。

Several scenarios of offline processing, incremental processing, fixed sliding receptive field, and event-based triggering receptive field can be implemented based on the choice of length and mechanism of indicating the receptive field. We propose dynamic time wrapping as a mechanism that indicates a receptive field based on event triggering tactics. To evaluate the performance of this method in time series analysis, we applied the proposed layer in two publicly available datasets of PhysioNet competitions in 2015 and 2017 where the input data is ECG signal. We compared the performance of our method against a variety of known tuned methods from expert knowledge, machine learning, deep learning methods, and the combination of them. The proposed approach improves the state of the art in two known completions 2015 and 2017 around 4% and 7% correspondingly while it does not rely on in advance knowledge of the classes or the possible places of arrhythmia. 本文提出分段匹配层作为心电图（ECG）分类表示学习方法中的一个新颖层。尽管表示学习方法在时间序列分析中表现出色，但这些方法仍然面临一些挑战，包括方法结构复杂、解决方案缺乏通用性、需要专家知识和大规模训练数据集。我们引入了基于两个级别的分段匹配层来解决上述一些挑战。在第一级，根据每个周期部分及其邻居计算一组形态、统计和频率特征及其比较形式。在第二级，这些特征通过基于感受野场景的预定义转换函数进行修改。根据感受野长度和指示机制的选择，可以实现离线处理、增量处理、固定滑动感受野、基于事件触发感受野等几种场景。我们提出动态时间包裹作为一种机制，指示基于事件触发策略的感受野。为了评估该方法在时间序列分析中的性能，我们在 2015 年和 2017 年 PhysioNet 竞赛的两个公开数据集中应用了所提出的层，其中输入数据是心电图信号。我们将我们的方法的性能与来自专家知识、机器学习、深度学习方法及其组合的各种已知调整方法进行了比较。所提出的方法将 2015 年和 2017 年两个已知完成的现有技术水平相应提高了约 4% 和 7%，同时它不依赖于对心律失常的类别或可能位置的预先了解。 本文提出一种分段匹配层，用于心电图（ECG）分类的表示学习方法，以解决现有方法在复杂结构、通用性不足、依赖专家知识和大规模数据集等方面的挑战。 Behzad Ghazanfari,Fatemeh Afghah,Sixian Zhang eess.SP,cs.CV,cs.LG,eess.IV

authors_text: Pedro R. A. S. Bassi,Willian Rampazzo,Romis Attux
title: Transfer Learning and SpecAugment applied to SSVEP Based BCI Classification
title_zh: 迁移学习和 SpecAugment 应用于基于 SSVEP 的 BCI 分类
comment: ef:Biomedical Signal Processing and Control 67 (2021) 102542
comments: ef:Biomedical Signal Processing and Control 67 (2021) 102542
abstract: Objective: We used deep convolutional neural networks (DCNNs) to classify electroencephalography (EEG) signals in a steady-state visually evoked potentials (SSVEP) based single-channel brain-computer interface (BCI), which does not require calibration on the user. Methods: EEG signals were converted to spectrograms and served as input to train DCNNs using the transfer learning technique. We also modified and applied a data augmentation method, SpecAugment, generally employed for speech recognition. Furthermore, for comparison purposes, we classified the SSVEP dataset using Support-vector machines (SVMs) and Filter Bank canonical correlation analysis (FBCCA). Results: Excluding the evaluated user's data from the fine-tuning process, we reached 82.2% mean test accuracy and 0.825 mean F1-Score on 35 subjects from an open dataset, using a small data length (0.5 s), only one electrode (Oz) and the DCNN with transfer learning, window slicing (WS) and SpecAugment's time masks. Conclusion: The DCNN results surpassed SVM and FBCCA performances, using a single electrode and a small data length. Transfer learning provided minimal accuracy change, but made training faster. SpecAugment created a small performance improvement and was successfully combined with WS, yielding higher accuracies. Significance: We present a new methodology to solve the problem of SSVEP classification using DCNNs. We also modified a speech recognition data augmentation technique and applied it to the context of BCIs. The presented methodology surpassed performances obtained with FBCCA and SVMs (more traditional SSVEP classification methods) in BCIs with small data lengths and one electrode. This type of BCI can be used to develop small and fast systems.

Methods: EEG signals were converted to spectrograms and served as input to train DCNNs using the transfer learning technique. We also modified and applied a data augmentation method, SpecAugment, generally employed for speech recognition. Furthermore, for comparison purposes, we classified the SSVEP dataset using Support-vector machines (SVMs) and Filter Bank canonical correlation analysis (FBCCA). Results: Excluding the evaluated user's data from the fine-tuning process, we reached 82.2% mean test accuracy and 0.825 mean F1-Score on 35 subjects from an open dataset, using a small data length (0.5 s), only one electrode (Oz) and the DCNN with transfer learning, window slicing (WS) and SpecAugment's time masks. Conclusion: The DCNN results surpassed SVM and FBCCA performances, using a single electrode and a small data length. Transfer learning provided minimal accuracy change, but made training faster. SpecAugment created a small performance improvement and was successfully combined with WS, yielding higher accuracies. Significance: We present a new methodology to solve the problem of SSVEP classification using DCNNs. We also modified a speech recognition data augmentation technique and applied it to the context of BCIs. The presented methodology surpassed performances obtained with FBCCA and SVMs (more traditional SSVEP classification methods) in BCIs with small data lengths and one electrode. This type of BCI can be used to develop small and fast systems.
abstract_zh: 目的：我们使用深度卷积神经网络（DCNN）对基于稳态视觉诱发电位（SSVEP）的单通道脑机接口（BCI）中的脑电图（EEG）信号进行分类，这不需要用户进行校准。方法：将脑电图信号转换为频谱图，并作为使用迁移学习技术训练 DCNN 的输入。我们还修改并应用了通常用于语音识别的数据增强方法 SpecAugment。此外，出于比较目的，我们使用支持向量机 (SVM) 和滤波器组规范相关分析 (FBCCA) 对 SSVEP 数据集进行分类。结果：排除微调过程中评估的用户数据，我们使用小数据长度（0.5 秒）、仅一个电极（Oz）和具有迁移学习、窗口切片（WS）和 SpecAugment 时间掩模的 DCNN，在开放数据集中的 35 名受试者上达到了 82.2% 的平均测试准确度和 0.825 的平均 F1 分数。结论：使用单电极和较小的数据长度，DCNN 结果超越了 SVM 和 FBCCA 性能。迁移学习提供了最小的准确性变化，但使训练速度更快。 SpecAugment 带来了较小的性能改进，并成功与 WS 结合，产生了更高的精度。意义：我们提出了一种使用 DCNN 解决 SSVEP 分类问题的新方法。我们还修改了语音识别数据增强技术并将其应用到脑机接口的环境中。所提出的方法超越了在小数据长度和一个电极的 BCI 中使用 FBCCA 和 SVM（更传统的 SSVEP 分类方法）获得的性能。这种类型的 BCI 可用于开发小型且快速的系统。

AI.tldr: 本论文将深度卷积神经网络（DCNN）与迁移学习及SpecAugment技术结合，应用于基于稳态视觉诱发电位（SSVEP）的单通道脑机接口（BCI）分类，无需用户校准。
AI.motivation: 传统SSVEP分类方法（如SVM、FBCCA）需依赖大量数据或校准，而本文旨在通过迁移学习和数据增强技术，在小数据量、单电极条件下提升BCI分类性能。
AI.method: 将EEG信号转换为频谱图输入DCNN，采用迁移学习预训练模型；引入语音识别领域SpecAugment数据增强方法（时间掩码与窗口切片结合），并与SVM、FBCCA方法对比验证。
AI.result: 在35名受试者开放数据集上，使用0.5秒数据长度和单电极Oz，DCNN结合迁移学习与SpecAugment达到82.2%平均测试准确率和0.825平均F1值，优于SVM和FBCCA。
AI.conclusion: DCNN方法在单电极、小数据场景下显著优于传统方法，迁移学习加速训练且精度稳定，改进的SpecAugment增强策略有效提升分类性能，为小型化快速BCI系统提供新方案。

authors_text: Quan Huu Cap,Hiroki Tani,Hiroyuki Uga,Satoshi Kagiwada,Hitoshi Iyatomi
title: LASSR: Effective Super-Resolution Method for Plant Disease Diagnosis
title_zh: 激光：植物病害诊断的有效超分辨率方法
abstract: The collection of high-resolution training data is crucial in building robust plant disease diagnosis systems, since such data have a significant impact on diagnostic performance. However, they are very difficult to obtain and are not always available in practice. Deep learning-based techniques, and particularly generative adversarial networks (GANs), can be applied to generate high-quality super-resolution images, but these methods often produce unexpected artifacts that can lower the diagnostic performance. In this paper, we propose a novel artifact-suppression super-resolution method that is specifically designed for diagnosing leaf disease, called Leaf Artifact-Suppression Super Resolution (LASSR). Thanks to its own artifact removal module that detects and suppresses artifacts to a considerable extent, LASSR can generate much more pleasing, high-quality images compared to the state-of-the-art ESRGAN model. Experiments based on a five-class cucumber disease (including healthy) discrimination model show that training with data generated by LASSR significantly boosts the performance on an unseen test dataset by nearly 22% compared with the baseline, and that our approach is more than 2% better than a model trained with images generated by ESRGAN.
summary: The collection of high-resolution training data is crucial in building robust plant disease diagnosis systems, since such data have a significant impact on diagnostic performance. However, they are very difficult to obtain and are not always available in practice. Deep learning-based techniques, and particularly generative adversarial networks (GANs), can be applied to generate high-quality super-resolution images, but these methods often produce unexpected artifacts that can lower the diagnostic performance. In this paper, we propose a novel artifact-suppression super-resolution method that is specifically designed for diagnosing leaf disease, called Leaf Artifact-Suppression Super Resolution (LASSR). Thanks to its own artifact removal module that detects and suppresses artifacts to a considerable extent, LASSR can generate much more pleasing, high-quality images compared to the state-of-the-art ESRGAN model. Experiments based on a five-class cucumber disease (including healthy) discrimination model show that training with data generated by LASSR significantly boosts the performance on an unseen test dataset by nearly 22% compared with the baseline, and that our approach is more than 2% better than a model trained with images generated by ESRGAN.
abstract_zh: 高分辨率训练数据的收集对于构建强大的植物病害诊断系统至关重要，因为此类数据对诊断性能具有重大影响。然而，它们非常难以获得，并且在实践中并不总是可用。基于深度学习的技术，特别是生成对抗网络（GAN），可用于生成高质量的超分辨率图像，但这些方法通常会产生意想不到的伪影，从而降低诊断性能。在本文中，我们提出了一种专门用于诊断叶病的新型伪影抑制超分辨率方法，称为叶伪影抑制超分辨率（LASSR）。由于其自己的伪影去除模块可以在很大程度上检测和抑制伪影，与最先进的 ESRGAN 模型相比，LASSR 可以生成更令人愉悦的高质量图像。基于五类黄瓜疾病（包括健康）判别模型的实验表明，使用 LASSR 生成的数据进行训练，与基线相比，在未见过的测试数据集上的性能显着提高了近 22%，并且我们的方法比使用 ESRGAN 生成的图像训练的模型要好 2% 以上。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为LASSR的超分辨率方法，专门用于提升植物疾病诊断中低分辨率图像的质量，通过抑制生成图像中的伪影来提高诊断准确性。
AI.motivation: 高分辨率图像对植物疾病诊断至关重要，但实际中难以获取。现有GAN生成的超分辨率图像常包含伪影，可能降低诊断性能，因此需要一种能有效抑制伪影的超分辨率方法。
AI.method: 设计LASSR方法，包含专门的伪影去除模块，通过检测并抑制生成图像中的伪影，生成更高质量的超分辨率图像，特别针对叶部疾病诊断优化。
AI.result: 实验表明，使用LASSR生成的图像训练的五类黄瓜病害分类模型，在测试集上性能提升近22%，相比ESRGAN方法提升超过2%。
AI.conclusion: LASSR在植物疾病诊断场景中能有效生成高质量超分辨率图像，显著提升诊断性能，且优于现有主流超分辨率方法ESRGAN。

motivation: 高分辨率图像对植物疾病诊断至关重要，但实际中难以获取。现有GAN生成的超分辨率图像常包含伪影，可能降低诊断性能，因此需要一种能有效抑制伪影的超分辨率方法。
method: 设计LASSR方法，包含专门的伪影去除模块，通过检测并抑制生成图像中的伪影，生成更高质量的超分辨率图像，特别针对叶部疾病诊断优化。
result: 实验表明，使用LASSR生成的图像训练的五类黄瓜病害分类模型，在测试集上性能提升近22%，相比ESRGAN方法提升超过2%。
conclusion: LASSR在植物疾病诊断场景中能有效生成高质量超分辨率图像，显著提升诊断性能，且优于现有主流超分辨率方法ESRGAN。
search_text: LASSR: Effective Super-Resolution Method for Plant Disease Diagnosis 激光：植物病害诊断的有效超分辨率方法 The collection of high-resolution training data is crucial in building robust plant disease diagnosis systems, since such data have a significant impact on diagnostic performance. However, they are very difficult to obtain and are not always available in practice. Deep learning-based techniques, and particularly generative adversarial networks (GANs), can be applied to generate high-quality super-resolution images, but these methods often produce unexpected artifacts that can lower the diagnostic performance. In this paper, we propose a novel artifact-suppression super-resolution method that is specifically designed for diagnosing leaf disease, called Leaf Artifact-Suppression Super Resolution (LASSR). Thanks to its own artifact removal module that detects and suppresses artifacts to a considerable extent, LASSR can generate much more pleasing, high-quality images compared to the state-of-the-art ESRGAN model. Experiments based on a five-class cucumber disease (including healthy) discrimination model show that training with data generated by LASSR significantly boosts the performance on an unseen test dataset by nearly 22% compared with the baseline, and that our approach is more than 2% better than a model trained with images generated by ESRGAN. 高分辨率训练数据的收集对于构建强大的植物病害诊断系统至关重要，因为此类数据对诊断性能具有重大影响。然而，它们非常难以获得，并且在实践中并不总是可用。基于深度学习的技术，特别是生成对抗网络（GAN），可用于生成高质量的超分辨率图像，但这些方法通常会产生意想不到的伪影，从而降低诊断性能。在本文中，我们提出了一种专门用于诊断叶病的新型伪影抑制超分辨率方法，称为叶伪影抑制超分辨率（LASSR）。由于其自己的伪影去除模块可以在很大程度上检测和抑制伪影，与最先进的 ESRGAN 模型相比，LASSR 可以生成更令人愉悦的高质量图像。基于五类黄瓜疾病（包括健康）判别模型的实验表明，使用 LASSR 生成的数据进行训练，与基线相比，在未见过的测试数据集上的性能显着提高了近 22%，并且我们的方法比使用 ESRGAN 生成的图像训练的模型要好 2% 以上。 本文提出一种名为LASSR的超分辨率方法，专门用于提升植物疾病诊断中低分辨率图像的质量，通过抑制生成图像中的伪影来提高诊断准确性。 Quan Huu Cap,Hiroki Tani,Hiroyuki Uga,Satoshi Kagiwada,Hitoshi Iyatomi cs.CV,eess.IV

authors_text: Mark Pritt,Gary Chern
title: Satellite Image Classification with Deep Learning
title_zh: 利用深度学习进行卫星图像分类
comment: 7 pages, 18 figures, 2017 IEEE Applied Imagery Pattern Recognition Workshop (AIPR)
comments: 7 pages, 18 figures, 2017 IEEE Applied Imagery Pattern Recognition Workshop (AIPR)
abstract: Satellite imagery is important for many applications including disaster response, law enforcement, and environmental monitoring. These applications require the manual identification of objects and facilities in the imagery. Because the geographic expanses to be covered are great and the analysts available to conduct the searches are few, automation is required. Yet traditional object detection and classification algorithms are too inaccurate and unreliable to solve the problem. Deep learning is a family of machine learning algorithms that have shown promise for the automation of such tasks. It has achieved success in image understanding by means of convolutional neural networks. In this paper we apply them to the problem of object and facility recognition in high-resolution, multi-spectral satellite imagery. We describe a deep learning system for classifying objects and facilities from the IARPA Functional Map of the World (fMoW) dataset into 63 different classes. The system consists of an ensemble of convolutional neural networks and additional neural networks that integrate satellite metadata with image features. It is implemented in Python using the Keras and TensorFlow deep learning libraries and runs on a Linux server with an NVIDIA Titan X graphics card. At the time of writing the system is in 2nd place in the fMoW TopCoder competition. Its total accuracy is 83%, the F1 score is 0.797, and it classifies 15 of the classes with accuracies of 95% or better.
summary: Satellite imagery is important for many applications including disaster response, law enforcement, and environmental monitoring. These applications require the manual identification of objects and facilities in the imagery. Because the geographic expanses to be covered are great and the analysts available to conduct the searches are few, automation is required. Yet traditional object detection and classification algorithms are too inaccurate and unreliable to solve the problem. Deep learning is a family of machine learning algorithms that have shown promise for the automation of such tasks. It has achieved success in image understanding by means of convolutional neural networks. In this paper we apply them to the problem of object and facility recognition in high-resolution, multi-spectral satellite imagery. We describe a deep learning system for classifying objects and facilities from the IARPA Functional Map of the World (fMoW) dataset into 63 different classes. The system consists of an ensemble of convolutional neural networks and additional neural networks that integrate satellite metadata with image features. It is implemented in Python using the Keras and TensorFlow deep learning libraries and runs on a Linux server with an NVIDIA Titan X graphics card. At the time of writing the system is in 2nd place in the fMoW TopCoder competition. Its total accuracy is 83%, the F1 score is 0.797, and it classifies 15 of the classes with accuracies of 95% or better.
abstract_zh: 卫星图像对于灾难响应、执法和环境监测等许多应用都很重要。这些应用程序需要手动识别图像中的物体和设施。由于要覆盖的地理范围很大，而能够进行搜索的分析师很少，因此需要自动化。然而传统的目标检测和分类算法太不准确且不可靠，无法解决该问题。深度学习是一系列机器学习算法，已显示出实现此类任务自动化的希望。它通过卷积神经网络在图像理解方面取得了成功。在本文中，我们将它们应用于高分辨率、多光谱卫星图像中的物体和设施识别问题。我们描述了一个深度学习系统，用于将 IARPA 世界功能地图 (fMoW) 数据集中的对象和设施分类为 63 个不同的类别。该系统由卷积神经网络和附加神经网络组成，将卫星元数据与图像特征集成在一起。它使用 Keras 和 TensorFlow 深度学习库在 Python 中实现，并在配备 NVIDIA Titan X 显卡的 Linux 服务器上运行。截至撰写本文时，该系统在 fMoW TopCoder 竞赛中排名第二。其总准确率为 83%，F1 分数为 0.797，对其中 15 个类进行分类，准确率达到 95% 或更高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的高分辨率多光谱卫星图像分类系统，用于识别63种不同类别的物体和设施。
AI.motivation: 卫星图像在灾害响应、执法和环境监测等领域具有重要应用，但传统算法精度不足，且人工识别效率低，亟需自动化解决方案。
AI.method: 采用卷积神经网络（CNN）集成系统，结合卫星元数据与图像特征，使用Keras和TensorFlow框架在Linux服务器上实现。
AI.result: 系统在fMoW数据集上总准确率为83%，F1得分为0.797，其中15个类别准确率超过95%，并在fMoW TopCoder竞赛中排名第二。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习方法显著提升了卫星图像分类的准确性，验证了CNN在复杂场景下的有效性，未来可进一步优化模型结构与数据融合策略。

These applications require the manual identification of objects and facilities in the imagery. Because the geographic expanses to be covered are great and the analysts available to conduct the searches are few, automation is required. Yet traditional object detection and classification algorithms are too inaccurate and unreliable to solve the problem. Deep learning is a family of machine learning algorithms that have shown promise for the automation of such tasks. It has achieved success in image understanding by means of convolutional neural networks. The system consists of an ensemble of convolutional neural networks and additional neural networks that integrate satellite metadata with image features. It is implemented in Python using the Keras and TensorFlow deep learning libraries and runs on a Linux server with an NVIDIA Titan X graphics card. authors_text: Brian Ichter,Pierre Sermanet,Corey Lynch
title: Broadly-Exploring, Local-Policy Trees for Long-Horizon Task Planning
title_zh: 用于长期任务规划的广泛探索的局部政策树
abstract: Long-horizon planning in realistic environments requires the ability to reason over sequential tasks in high-dimensional state spaces with complex dynamics. Classical motion planning algorithms, such as rapidly-exploring random trees, are capable of efficiently exploring large state spaces and computing long-horizon, sequential plans. However, these algorithms are generally challenged with complex, stochastic, and high-dimensional state spaces as well as in the presence of narrow passages, which naturally emerge in tasks that interact with the environment. Machine learning offers a promising solution for its ability to learn general policies that can handle complex interactions and high-dimensional observations. However, these policies are generally limited in horizon length. Our approach, Broadly-Exploring, Local-policy Trees (BELT), merges these two approaches to leverage the strengths of both through a task-conditioned, model-based tree search. BELT uses an RRT-inspired tree search to efficiently explore the state space. Locally, the exploration is guided by a task-conditioned, learned policy capable of performing general short-horizon tasks. This task space can be quite general and abstract; its only requirements are to be sampleable and to well-cover the space of useful tasks. This search is aided by a task-conditioned model that temporally extends dynamics propagation to allow long-horizon search and sequential reasoning over tasks. BELT is demonstrated experimentally to be able to plan long-horizon, sequential trajectories with a goal conditioned policy and generate plans that are robust.
summary: Long-horizon planning in realistic environments requires the ability to reason over sequential tasks in high-dimensional state spaces with complex dynamics. Classical motion planning algorithms, such as rapidly-exploring random trees, are capable of efficiently exploring large state spaces and computing long-horizon, sequential plans. However, these algorithms are generally challenged with complex, stochastic, and high-dimensional state spaces as well as in the presence of narrow passages, which naturally emerge in tasks that interact with the environment. Machine learning offers a promising solution for its ability to learn general policies that can handle complex interactions and high-dimensional observations. However, these policies are generally limited in horizon length. Our approach, Broadly-Exploring, Local-policy Trees (BELT), merges these two approaches to leverage the strengths of both through a task-conditioned, model-based tree search. BELT uses an RRT-inspired tree search to efficiently explore the state space. Locally, the exploration is guided by a task-conditioned, learned policy capable of performing general short-horizon tasks. This task space can be quite general and abstract; its only requirements are to be sampleable and to well-cover the space of useful tasks. This search is aided by a task-conditioned model that temporally extends dynamics propagation to allow long-horizon search and sequential reasoning over tasks. BELT is demonstrated experimentally to be able to plan long-horizon, sequential trajectories with a goal conditioned policy and generate plans that are robust.
abstract_zh: 现实环境中的长期规划需要能够对具有复杂动态的高维状态空间中的顺序任务进行推理。经典的运动规划算法，例如快速探索随机树，能够有效地探索大型状态空间并计算长范围的顺序计划。然而，这些算法通常面临复杂、随机和高维状态空间以及狭窄通道的挑战，这些通道自然出现在与环境交互的任务中。机器学习提供了一个有前途的解决方案，因为它能够学习能够处理复杂交互和高维观察的一般策略。然而，这些政策的期限通常受到限制。我们的方法，广泛探索，局部策略树（BELT），融合了这两种方法，通过任务条件、基于模型的树搜索来利用两者的优势。 BELT 使用受 RRT 启发的树搜索来有效地探索状态空间。在本地，探索是由能够执行一般短期任务的任务条件学习策略引导的。这个任务空间可能非常笼统和抽象；它唯一的要求是可采样并很好地覆盖有用任务的空间。这种搜索得到了任务条件模型的帮助，该模型暂时扩展了动态传播，以允许对任务进行长范围搜索和顺序推理。实验证明，BELT 能够利用目标条件策略来规划长期、连续的轨迹，并生成稳健的计划。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合经典运动规划与机器学习策略的长时序任务规划方法BELT，以解决复杂高维环境中的长期规划问题。
AI.motivation: 经典规划算法在复杂动态环境和狭窄通道中表现不足，而机器学习策略的时序长度有限，需融合两者优势以实现鲁棒的长期规划。
AI.method: BELT采用RRT启发的树搜索结构，通过任务条件化策略指导局部探索，并利用任务条件化模型扩展动力学传播以支持长期时序推理。
AI.result: 实验表明BELT能生成鲁棒的长期目标条件化轨迹规划方案，有效结合了树搜索的探索效率与策略模型的复杂任务处理能力。
AI.conclusion: BELT通过融合经典规划与机器学习策略，为复杂环境下的长时序任务规划提供了高效且鲁棒的解决方案。

search_text: Broadly-Exploring, Local-Policy Trees for Long-Horizon Task Planning 用于长期任务规划的广泛探索的局部政策树 Long-horizon planning in realistic environments requires the ability to reason over sequential tasks in high-dimensional state spaces with complex dynamics. Classical motion planning algorithms, such as rapidly-exploring random trees, are capable of efficiently exploring large state spaces and computing long-horizon, sequential plans. However, these algorithms are generally challenged with complex, stochastic, and high-dimensional state spaces as well as in the presence of narrow passages, which naturally emerge in tasks that interact with the environment. Machine learning offers a promising solution for its ability to learn general policies that can handle complex interactions and high-dimensional observations. However, these policies are generally limited in horizon length. Our approach, Broadly-Exploring, Local-policy Trees (BELT), merges these two approaches to leverage the strengths of both through a task-conditioned, model-based tree search. BELT uses an RRT-inspired tree search to efficiently explore the state space. Locally, the exploration is guided by a task-conditioned, learned policy capable of performing general short-horizon tasks. This task space can be quite general and abstract; its only requirements are to be sampleable and to well-cover the space of useful tasks. This search is aided by a task-conditioned model that temporally extends dynamics propagation to allow long-horizon search and sequential reasoning over tasks. BELT is demonstrated experimentally to be able to plan long-horizon, sequential trajectories with a goal conditioned policy and generate plans that are robust. 现实环境中的长期规划需要能够对具有复杂动态的高维状态空间中的顺序任务进行推理。经典的运动规划算法，例如快速探索随机树，能够有效地探索大型状态空间并计算长范围的顺序计划。然而，这些算法通常面临复杂、随机和高维状态空间以及狭窄通道的挑战，这些通道自然出现在与环境交互的任务中。机器学习提供了一个有前途的解决方案，因为它能够学习能够处理复杂交互和高维观察的一般策略。然而，这些政策的期限通常受到限制。我们的方法，广泛探索，局部策略树（BELT），融合了这两种方法，通过任务条件、基于模型的树搜索来利用两者的优势。 BELT 使用受 RRT 启发的树搜索来有效地探索状态空间。在本地，探索是由能够执行一般短期任务的任务条件学习策略引导的。这个任务空间可能非常笼统和抽象；它唯一的要求是可采样并很好地覆盖有用任务的空间。这种搜索得到了任务条件模型的帮助，该模型暂时扩展了动态传播，以允许对任务进行长范围搜索和顺序推理。实验证明，BELT 能够利用目标条件策略来规划长期、连续的轨迹，并生成稳健的计划。 本文提出了一种结合经典运动规划与机器学习策略的长时序任务规划方法BELT，以解决复杂高维环境中的长期规划问题。 Brian Ichter,Pierre Sermanet,Corey Lynch cs.RO,cs.LG

authors_text: Michael Emmerich,Joost Nibbeling,Marios Kefalas,Aske Plaat
title: Multiple Node Immunisation for Preventing Epidemics on Networks by Exact Multiobjective Optimisation of Cost and Shield-Value
title_zh: 通过成本和屏蔽值的精确多目标优化来预防网络流行病的多节点免疫
comment: Based on the Master Thesis of Joost Nibbeling, LIACS, Leiden University, The Netherlands
comments: Based on the Master Thesis of Joost Nibbeling, LIACS, Leiden University, The Netherlands
abstract: The general problem in this paper is vertex (node) subset selection with the goal to contain an infection that spreads in a network. Instead of selecting the single most important node, this paper deals with the problem of selecting multiple nodes for removal. As compared to previous work on multiple-node selection, the trade-off between cost and benefit is considered. The benefit is measured in terms of increasing the epidemic threshold which is a measure of how difficult it is for an infection to spread in a network. The cost is measured in terms of the number and size of nodes to be removed or controlled. Already in its single-objective instance with a fixed number of $k$ nodes to be removed, the multiple vertex immunisation problems have been proven to be NP-hard. Several heuristics have been developed to approximate the problem. In this work, we compare meta-heuristic techniques with exact methods on the Shield-value, which is a sub-modular proxy for the maximal eigenvalue and used in the current state-of-the-art greedy node-removal strategies. We generalise it to the multi-objective case and replace the greedy algorithm by a quadratic program (QP), which then can be solved with exact QP solvers. The main contribution of this paper is the insight that, if time permits, exact and problem-specific methods approximation should be used, which are often far better than Pareto front approximations obtained by general meta-heuristics. Based on these, it will be more effective to develop strategies for controlling real-world networks when the goal is to prevent or contain epidemic outbreaks. This paper is supported by ready to use Python implementation of the optimization methods and datasets.

The benefit is measured in terms of increasing the epidemic threshold which is a measure of how difficult it is for an infection to spread in a network. The cost is measured in terms of the number and size of nodes to be removed or controlled. Already in its single-objective instance with a fixed number of $k$ nodes to be removed, the multiple vertex immunisation problems have been proven to be NP-hard. Several heuristics have been developed to approximate the problem. In this work, we compare meta-heuristic techniques with exact methods on the Shield-value, which is a sub-modular proxy for the maximal eigenvalue and used in the current state-of-the-art greedy node-removal strategies. We generalise it to the multi-objective case and replace the greedy algorithm by a quadratic program (QP), which then can be solved with exact QP solvers. The main contribution of this paper is the insight that, if time permits, exact and problem-specific methods approximation should be used, which are often far better than Pareto front approximations obtained by general meta-heuristics. Based on these, it will be more effective to develop strategies for controlling real-world networks when the goal is to prevent or contain epidemic outbreaks. This paper is supported by ready to use Python implementation of the optimization methods and datasets.
abstract_zh: 本文的一般问题是顶点（节点）子集选择，其目标是遏制在网络中传播的感染。本文不是选择单个最重要的节点，而是处理选择多个节点进行删除的问题。与之前的多节点选择工作相比，考虑了成本和收益之间的权衡。其好处是通过提高流行阈值来衡量的，该阈值衡量感染在网络中传播的难度。成本是根据要删除或控制的节点的数量和大小来衡量的。在单目标实例中，要删除固定数量的 $k$ 节点，多顶点免疫问题已被证明是 NP 困难的。已经开发了几种启发式方法来近似该问题。在这项工作中，我们将元启发式技术与屏蔽值的精确方法进行比较，屏蔽值是最大特征值的子模块代理，并用于当前最先进的贪婪节点删除策略。我们将其推广到多目标情况，并用二次规划 (QP) 代替贪婪算法，然后可以使用精确的 QP 求解器进行求解。本文的主要贡献在于，如果时间允许，应该使用精确且针对具体问题的方法逼近，这通常比一般元启发式方法获得的帕累托前沿逼近要好得多。基于这些，当目标是预防或遏制流行病爆发时，制定控制现实世界网络的策略将更加有效。本文得到了优化方法和数据集的现成 Python 实现的支持。

AI.tldr: 本文研究通过精确多目标优化方法选择多个节点进行免疫，以在网络中防止疫情传播。
AI.motivation: 传统方法多关注单个关键节点，而实际中需权衡移除多个节点的成本与收益，现有启发式方法可能无法达到最优。
AI.method: 将多目标优化问题转化为二次规划（QP）模型，利用精确求解器替代贪心算法，并与元启发式方法对比。
AI.result: 精确方法在时间允许时显著优于通用元启发式方法的帕累托前沿近似，优化效果更优。
AI.conclusion: 针对疫情控制的网络策略，应优先采用精确且问题特定的优化方法以提升实际效果。

authors_text: Clemens-Alexander Brust,Björn Barz,Joachim Denzler
title: Making Every Label Count: Handling Semantic Imprecision by Integrating Domain Knowledge
title_zh: 让每个标签都发挥作用：通过整合领域知识处理语义不精确问题
comment: 9 pages pre-print. Accepted for publication at ICPR 2020
comments: 9 pages pre-print. Accepted for publication at ICPR 2020
abstract: Noisy data, crawled from the web or supplied by volunteers such as Mechanical Turkers or citizen scientists, is considered an alternative to professionally labeled data. There has been research focused on mitigating the effects of label noise. It is typically modeled as inaccuracy, where the correct label is replaced by an incorrect label from the same set. We consider an additional dimension of label noise: imprecision. For example, a non-breeding snow bunting is labeled as a bird. This label is correct, but not as precise as the task requires. Standard softmax classifiers cannot learn from such a weak label because they consider all classes mutually exclusive, which non-breeding snow bunting and bird are not. We propose CHILLAX (Class Hierarchies for Imprecise Label Learning and Annotation eXtrapolation), a method based on hierarchical classification, to fully utilize labels of any precision. Experiments on noisy variants of NABirds and ILSVRC2012 show that our method outperforms strong baselines by as much as 16.4 percentage points, and the current state of the art by up to 3.9 percentage points.
summary: Noisy data, crawled from the web or supplied by volunteers such as Mechanical Turkers or citizen scientists, is considered an alternative to professionally labeled data. There has been research focused on mitigating the effects of label noise. It is typically modeled as inaccuracy, where the correct label is replaced by an incorrect label from the same set. We consider an additional dimension of label noise: imprecision. For example, a non-breeding snow bunting is labeled as a bird. This label is correct, but not as precise as the task requires. Standard softmax classifiers cannot learn from such a weak label because they consider all classes mutually exclusive, which non-breeding snow bunting and bird are not. We propose CHILLAX (Class Hierarchies for Imprecise Label Learning and Annotation eXtrapolation), a method based on hierarchical classification, to fully utilize labels of any precision. Experiments on noisy variants of NABirds and ILSVRC2012 show that our method outperforms strong baselines by as much as 16.4 percentage points, and the current state of the art by up to 3.9 percentage points.
abstract_zh: 从网络上爬取的或由 Mechanical Turkers 或公民科学家等志愿者提供的噪音数据被认为是专业标记数据的替代品。已有研究重点关注减轻标签噪声的影响。它通常被建模为不准确，即正确的标签被同一组中的错误标签替换。我们考虑标签噪声的另一个维度：不精确性。例如，非繁殖雪鹀被标记为鸟类。该标签是正确的，但不如任务要求的那么精确。标准的 softmax 分类器无法从如此弱的标签中学习，因为它们认为所有类别都是互斥的，而非繁殖的雪鹀和鸟类则不然。我们提出了CHILLAX（用于不精确标签学习和注释扩展的类层次结构），一种基于层次分类的方法，以充分利用任何精度的标签。对 NABirds 和 ILSVRC2012 的噪声变体进行的实验表明，我们的方法比强基线高出 16.4 个百分点，比当前最先进的方法高出 3.9 个百分点。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为CHILLAX的方法，通过整合领域知识处理标签语义不精确问题，提升分类模型性能。
AI.motivation: 现有标签噪声处理方法仅关注标签错误，而忽视了标签语义不精确（如将非繁殖期雪鹀标记为泛类'鸟'）导致的模型性能下降问题。
AI.method: 基于层次分类框架设计CHILLAX方法，通过构建类别层次结构建模标签间的语义关联，使模型能够利用不同精度的标签信息进行学习。
AI.result: 在NABirds和ILSVRC2012数据集的噪声变体实验中，该方法较强基线提升达16.4个百分点，较当前最优方法提升3.9个百分点。
AI.conclusion: 整合领域知识的层次分类方法能有效解决标签语义不精确问题，为利用弱标签数据训练模型提供了新思路。

Standard softmax classifiers cannot learn from such a weak label because they consider all classes mutually exclusive, which non-breeding snow bunting and bird are not. We propose CHILLAX (Class Hierarchies for Imprecise Label Learning and Annotation eXtrapolation), a method based on hierarchical classification, to fully utilize labels of any precision. Experiments on noisy variants of NABirds and ILSVRC2012 show that our method outperforms strong baselines by as much as 16.4 percentage points, and the current state of the art by up to 3.9 percentage points. 从网络上爬取的或由 Mechanical Turkers 或公民科学家等志愿者提供的噪音数据被认为是专业标记数据的替代品。已有研究重点关注减轻标签噪声的影响。它通常被建模为不准确，即正确的标签被同一组中的错误标签替换。我们考虑标签噪声的另一个维度：不精确性。例如，非繁殖雪鹀被标记为鸟类。该标签是正确的，但不如任务要求的那么精确。标准的 softmax 分类器无法从如此弱的标签中学习，因为它们认为所有类别都是互斥的，而非繁殖的雪鹀和鸟类则不然。我们提出了CHILLAX（用于不精确标签学习和注释扩展的类层次结构），一种基于层次分类的方法，以充分利用任何精度的标签。对 NABirds 和 ILSVRC2012 的噪声变体进行的实验表明，我们的方法比强基线高出 16.4 个百分点，比当前最先进的方法高出 3.9 个百分点。 本文提出了一种名为CHILLAX的方法，通过整合领域知识处理标签语义不精确问题，提升分类模型性能。 Clemens-Alexander Brust,Björn Barz,Joachim Denzler cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Gergely Hajgató,György Paál,Bálint Gyires-Tóth
title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Optimization of Pumps in Water Distribution Systems
title_zh: 用于供水系统中泵的实时优化的深度强化学习
comment: ef:Journal of Water Resources Planning and Management, Volume 146, Issue 11 (November 2020)
comments: ef:Journal of Water Resources Planning and Management, Volume 146, Issue 11 (November 2020)
abstract: Real-time control of pumps can be an infeasible task in water distribution systems (WDSs) because the calculation to find the optimal pump speeds is resource-intensive. The computational need cannot be lowered even with the capabilities of smart water networks when conventional optimization techniques are used. Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is presented here as a controller of pumps in two WDSs. An agent based on a dueling deep q-network is trained to maintain the pump speeds based on instantaneous nodal pressure data. General optimization techniques (e.g., Nelder-Mead method, differential evolution) serve as baselines. The total efficiency achieved by the DRL agent compared to the best performing baseline is above 0.98, whereas the speedup is around 2x compared to that. The main contribution of the presented approach is that the agent can run the pumps in real-time because it depends only on measurement data. If the WDS is replaced with a hydraulic simulation, the agent still outperforms conventional techniques in search speed.
summary: Real-time control of pumps can be an infeasible task in water distribution systems (WDSs) because the calculation to find the optimal pump speeds is resource-intensive. The computational need cannot be lowered even with the capabilities of smart water networks when conventional optimization techniques are used. Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is presented here as a controller of pumps in two WDSs. An agent based on a dueling deep q-network is trained to maintain the pump speeds based on instantaneous nodal pressure data. General optimization techniques (e.g., Nelder-Mead method, differential evolution) serve as baselines. The total efficiency achieved by the DRL agent compared to the best performing baseline is above 0.98, whereas the speedup is around 2x compared to that. The main contribution of the presented approach is that the agent can run the pumps in real-time because it depends only on measurement data. If the WDS is replaced with a hydraulic simulation, the agent still outperforms conventional techniques in search speed.
abstract_zh: 在配水系统 (WDS) 中，泵的实时控制可能是一项不可行的任务，因为寻找最佳泵速的计算需要消耗大量资源。当使用传统的优化技术时，即使具有智能水网络的功能，计算需求也无法降低。此处将深度强化学习 (DRL) 作为两个 WDS 中泵的控制器。基于决斗深度 q 网络的代理经过训练，可根据瞬时节点压力数据维持泵速。一般优化技术（例如 Nelder-Mead 方法、差分进化）作为基线。与最佳性能基线相比，DRL 代理实现的总效率高于 0.98，而加速比约为 2 倍。该方法的主要贡献是代理可以实时运行泵，因为它仅依赖于测量数据。如果将 WDS 替换为水力模拟，该代理在搜索速度方面仍然优于传统技术。

AI.tldr: 本文提出使用深度强化学习（DRL）实时优化供水系统中的水泵控制，以解决传统方法计算量大、难以实时处理的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统优化技术（如Nelder-Mead、差分进化）在供水系统水泵控制中计算资源消耗大，难以满足实时性需求，需更高效解决方案。
AI.method: 基于Dueling Deep Q-Network（DQN）的智能体，通过实时节点压力数据训练，直接控制水泵速度，与传统优化方法对比验证效果。
AI.result: DRL智能体效率达0.98以上，速度较传统方法提升2倍，且无需依赖液压模拟即可实现实时控制。
AI.conclusion: DRL在供水系统水泵控制中显著优于传统方法，其依赖实时测量数据的特性使其更适合实际工程场景的实时优化需求。

authors_text: Ewelina Weglarz-Tomczak,Jakub M. Tomczak,Agoston E. Eiben,Stanley Brul
title: Population-based Optimization for Kinetic Parameter Identification in Glycolytic Pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
title_zh: 基于群体的酿酒酵母糖酵解途径动力学参数识别优化
comment: Code at https://github.com/jmtomczak/popi
comments: Code at https://github.com/jmtomczak/popi
abstract: Models in systems biology are mathematical descriptions of biological processes that are used to answer questions and gain a better understanding of biological phenomena. Dynamic models represent the network through rates of the production and consumption for the individual species. The ordinary differential equations that describe rates of the reactions in the model include a set of parameters. The parameters are important quantities to understand and analyze biological systems. Moreover, the perturbation of the kinetic parameters are correlated with upregulation of the system by cell-intrinsic and cell-extrinsic factors, including mutations and the environment changes. Here, we aim at using well-established models of biological pathways to identify parameter values and point their potential perturbation/deviation. We present our population-based optimization framework that is able to identify kinetic parameters in the dynamic model based on only input and output data (i.e., timecourses of selected metabolites). Our approach can deal with the identification of the non-measurable parameters as well as with discovering deviation of the parameters. We present our proposed optimization framework on the example of the well-studied glycolytic pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
summary: Models in systems biology are mathematical descriptions of biological processes that are used to answer questions and gain a better understanding of biological phenomena. Dynamic models represent the network through rates of the production and consumption for the individual species. The ordinary differential equations that describe rates of the reactions in the model include a set of parameters. The parameters are important quantities to understand and analyze biological systems. Moreover, the perturbation of the kinetic parameters are correlated with upregulation of the system by cell-intrinsic and cell-extrinsic factors, including mutations and the environment changes. Here, we aim at using well-established models of biological pathways to identify parameter values and point their potential perturbation/deviation. We present our population-based optimization framework that is able to identify kinetic parameters in the dynamic model based on only input and output data (i.e., timecourses of selected metabolites). Our approach can deal with the identification of the non-measurable parameters as well as with discovering deviation of the parameters. We present our proposed optimization framework on the example of the well-studied glycolytic pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
abstract_zh: 系统生物学中的模型是生物过程的数学描述，用于回答问题并更好地理解生物现象。动态模型通过单个物种的生产和消费率来表示网络。描述模型中反应速率的常微分方程包括一组参数。这些参数是理解和分析生物系统的重要数量。此外，动力学参数的扰动与细胞内在和细胞外在因素（包括突变和环境变化）对系统的上调相关。在这里，我们的目标是使用完善的生物途径模型来识别参数值并指出它们的潜在扰动/偏差。我们提出了基于群体的优化框架，该框架能够仅基于输入和输出数据（即选定代谢物的时间进程）来识别动态模型中的动力学参数。我们的方法可以处理不可测量参数的识别以及发现参数的偏差。我们以酿酒酵母中经过充分研究的糖酵解途径为例，提出了我们提出的优化框架。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于种群的优化框架，用于识别酿酒酵母糖酵解途径模型中的动力学参数及其潜在扰动。
AI.motivation: 生物系统中的动力学参数对理解调控机制至关重要，但传统方法难以处理不可测量参数和环境因素导致的参数偏差，需开发新方法实现参数识别与扰动检测。
AI.method: 基于种群优化算法，仅利用输入输出数据（代谢物时间进程）识别动态模型中的非可测参数，并检测参数偏离情况。
AI.result: 在酿酒酵母糖酵解途径案例中验证了该框架的有效性，成功识别关键参数并发现潜在扰动模式。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为复杂生物系统的参数识别和扰动分析提供了可靠工具，具有推广至其他生物过程的潜力。

authors_text: Jian Mei,Hao Dong
title: The DongNiao International Birds 10000 Dataset
title_zh: 冬鸟国际鸟类10000只数据集
abstract: DongNiao International Birds 10000 (DIB-10K) is a challenging image dataset which has more than 10 thousand different types of birds. It was created to enable the study of machine learning and also ornithology research. DIB-10K does not own the copyright of these images. It only provides thumbnails of images, in a way similar to ImageNet.
summary: DongNiao International Birds 10000 (DIB-10K) is a challenging image dataset which has more than 10 thousand different types of birds. It was created to enable the study of machine learning and also ornithology research. DIB-10K does not own the copyright of these images. It only provides thumbnails of images, in a way similar to ImageNet.
abstract_zh: 冬鸟国际鸟类 10000 (DIB-10K) 是一个具有挑战性的图像数据集，其中包含 10,000 多种不同类型的鸟类。它的创建是为了促进机器学习和鸟类学研究。 DIB-10K 不拥有这些图像的版权。它只提供图像的缩略图，类似于 ImageNet。

AI.tldr: 创建包含超过1万种鸟类图像的DongNiao International Birds 10000 (DIB-10K) 数据集，用于支持机器学习和鸟类学研究。
AI.motivation: 为机器学习算法训练和鸟类学研究提供多样化、大规模的鸟类图像资源，推动相关领域的发展。
AI.method: 收集超过1万种鸟类的图像，生成缩略图并整理为数据集，类似ImageNet的结构，但不包含原始图像版权。
AI.result: 成功构建了DIB-10K数据集，可作为机器学习模型训练和鸟类分类研究的基准数据集。
AI.conclusion: DIB-10K数据集为鸟类相关研究提供了重要资源，其缩略图形式和多样性有助于算法优化与生物学分析。

search_text: The DongNiao International Birds 10000 Dataset 冬鸟国际鸟类10000只数据集 DongNiao International Birds 10000 (DIB-10K) is a challenging image dataset which has more than 10 thousand different types of birds. It was created to enable the study of machine learning and also ornithology research. DIB-10K does not own the copyright of these images. It only provides thumbnails of images, in a way similar to ImageNet. 冬鸟国际鸟类 10000 (DIB-10K) 是一个具有挑战性的图像数据集，其中包含 10,000 多种不同类型的鸟类。它的创建是为了促进机器学习和鸟类学研究。 DIB-10K 不拥有这些图像的版权。它只提供图像的缩略图，类似于 ImageNet。 创建包含超过1万种鸟类图像的DongNiao International Birds 10000 (DIB-10K) 数据集，用于支持机器学习和鸟类学研究。 Jian Mei,Hao Dong cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Tarik Ayaou,Mourad Boussaid,Karim Afdel,Abdellah Amghar
title: Improving Road Signs Detection performance by Combining the Features of Hough Transform and Texture
title_zh: 结合霍夫变换和纹理特征提高路标检测性能
comment: 6 pages
comments: 6 pages
abstract: With the large uses of the intelligent systems in different domains, and in order to increase the drivers and pedestrians safety, the road and traffic sign recognition system has been a challenging issue and an important task for many years. But studies, done in this field of detection and recognition of traffic signs in an image, which are interested in the Arab context, are still insufficient. Detection of the road signs present in the scene is the one of the main stages of the traffic sign detection and recognition. In this paper, an efficient solution to enhance road signs detection, including Arabic context, performance based on color segmentation, Randomized Hough Transform and the combination of Zernike moments and Haralick features has been made. Segmentation stage is useful to determine the Region of Interest (ROI) in the image. The Randomized Hough Transform (RHT) is used to detect the circular and octagonal shapes. This stage is improved by the extraction of the Haralick features and Zernike moments. Furthermore, we use it as input of a classifier based on SVM. Experimental results show that the proposed approach allows us to perform the measurements precision.
summary: With the large uses of the intelligent systems in different domains, and in order to increase the drivers and pedestrians safety, the road and traffic sign recognition system has been a challenging issue and an important task for many years. But studies, done in this field of detection and recognition of traffic signs in an image, which are interested in the Arab context, are still insufficient. Detection of the road signs present in the scene is the one of the main stages of the traffic sign detection and recognition. In this paper, an efficient solution to enhance road signs detection, including Arabic context, performance based on color segmentation, Randomized Hough Transform and the combination of Zernike moments and Haralick features has been made. Segmentation stage is useful to determine the Region of Interest (ROI) in the image. The Randomized Hough Transform (RHT) is used to detect the circular and octagonal shapes. This stage is improved by the extraction of the Haralick features and Zernike moments. Furthermore, we use it as input of a classifier based on SVM. Experimental results show that the proposed approach allows us to perform the measurements precision.
abstract_zh: 随着智能系统在不同领域的大量使用，为了提高驾驶员和行人的安全，道路和交通标志识别系统多年来一直是一个具有挑战性的问题和重要任务。但在图像中交通标志的检测和识别领域所做的研究（对阿拉伯背景感兴趣）仍然不足。检测场景中存在的道路标志是交通标志检测和识别的主要阶段之一。本文提出了一种增强路标检测的有效解决方案，包括阿拉伯语上下文、基于颜色分割的性能、随机霍夫变换以及Zernike矩和Haralick特征的组合。分割阶段对于确定图像中的感兴趣区域 (ROI) 很有用。随机霍夫变换 (RHT) 用于检测圆形和八边形形状。该阶段通过提取 Haralick 特征和 Zernike 矩进行改进。此外，我们将其用作基于 SVM 的分类器的输入。实验结果表明，所提出的方法使我们能够进行精确的测量。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合霍夫变换和纹理特征的方法，以提高道路标志检测的性能，特别是在阿拉伯语环境下的应用。
AI.motivation: 由于智能系统在不同领域的广泛应用，为了提高驾驶员和行人的安全性，交通标志识别系统成为了一个重要的研究课题。然而，针对阿拉伯语环境下的交通标志检测研究仍显不足。
AI.method: 该方法基于颜色分割、随机霍夫变换（RHT）以及Zernike矩和Haralick特征的组合。首先通过颜色分割确定图像中的感兴趣区域（ROI），然后利用RHT检测圆形和八边形形状，并通过提取Haralick特征和Zernike矩进行改进，最后将这些特征输入支持向量机（SVM）分类器进行分类。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，所提出的方法能够有效提高道路标志检测的精度。
AI.conclusion: 结合霍夫变换和纹理特征的方法能够有效提升道路标志检测的性能，特别是在阿拉伯语环境下的应用具有重要意义。

authors_text: Yann Desmarais,Denis Mottet,Pierre Slangen,Philippe Montesinos
title: A review of 3D human pose estimation algorithms for markerless motion capture
title_zh: 无标记运动捕捉的 3D 人体姿态估计算法综述
comment: 25 pages, 3 figures
comments: 25 pages, 3 figures
abstract: Human pose estimation is a very active research field, stimulated by its important applications in robotics, entertainment or health and sports sciences, among others. Advances in convolutional networks triggered noticeable improvements in 2D pose estimation, leading modern 3D markerless motion capture techniques to an average error per joint of 20 mm. However, with the proliferation of methods, it is becoming increasingly difficult to make an informed choice. Here, we review the leading human pose estimation methods of the past five years, focusing on metrics, benchmarks and method structures. We propose a taxonomy based on accuracy, speed and robustness that we use to classify de methods and derive directions for future research.
summary: Human pose estimation is a very active research field, stimulated by its important applications in robotics, entertainment or health and sports sciences, among others. Advances in convolutional networks triggered noticeable improvements in 2D pose estimation, leading modern 3D markerless motion capture techniques to an average error per joint of 20 mm. However, with the proliferation of methods, it is becoming increasingly difficult to make an informed choice. Here, we review the leading human pose estimation methods of the past five years, focusing on metrics, benchmarks and method structures. We propose a taxonomy based on accuracy, speed and robustness that we use to classify de methods and derive directions for future research.
abstract_zh: 人体姿势估计是一个非常活跃的研究领域，受到其在机器人、娱乐或健康和运动科学等领域的重要应用的推动。卷积网络的进步引发了 2D 姿态估计的显着改进，使现代 3D 无标记运动捕捉技术的每个关节的平均误差为 20 毫米。然而，随着方法的激增，做出明智的选择变得越来越困难。在这里，我们回顾了过去五年中领先的人体姿势估计方法，重点关注指标、基准和方法结构。我们提出了一种基于准确性、速度和稳健性的分类法，用于对方法进行分类并得出未来研究的方向。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了过去五年用于无标记运动捕捉的3D人体姿态估计算法，分析其指标、基准和结构，并提出基于准确性、速度和鲁棒性的分类方法。
AI.motivation: 随着2D姿态估计技术进步，3D无标记运动捕捉方法激增，但缺乏系统分类框架，导致难以选择合适算法并明确研究方向。
AI.method: 通过回顾近五年主流算法，建立以准确性、速度和鲁棒性为核心的分类体系，分析各方法在基准数据集上的表现差异。
AI.result: 提出三维分类框架，总结当前技术瓶颈，并指出提升鲁棒性与实时性、融合多模态数据为未来研究重点。
AI.conclusion: 该分类体系为算法选择和研发提供指导，强调需平衡精度与效率，推动3D姿态估计在医疗、VR等场景的实际应用。

authors_text: Shujun Wang,Yaxi Zhu,Lequan Yu,Hao Chen,Huangjing Lin,Xiangbo Wan,Xinjuan Fan,Pheng-Ann Hen
title: RMDL: Recalibrated multi-instance deep learning for whole slide gastric image classification
title_zh: RMDL：重新校准的多实例深度学习，用于整个幻灯片胃图像分类
comment: Accepted at Medical Image Analysis. Code: https://github.com/EmmaW8/RMDL
comments: Accepted at Medical Image Analysis. Code: https://github.com/EmmaW8/RMDL
abstract: The whole slide histopathology images (WSIs) play a critical role in gastric cancer diagnosis. However, due to the large scale of WSIs and various sizes of the abnormal area, how to select informative regions and analyze them are quite challenging during the automatic diagnosis process. The multi-instance learning based on the most discriminative instances can be of great benefit for whole slide gastric image diagnosis. In this paper, we design a recalibrated multi-instance deep learning method (RMDL) to address this challenging problem. We first select the discriminative instances, and then utilize these instances to diagnose diseases based on the proposed RMDL approach. The designed RMDL network is capable of capturing instance-wise dependencies and recalibrating instance features according to the importance coefficient learned from the fused features. Furthermore, we build a large whole-slide gastric histopathology image dataset with detailed pixel-level annotations. Experimental results on the constructed gastric dataset demonstrate the significant improvement on the accuracy of our proposed framework compared with other state-of-the-art multi-instance learning methods. Moreover, our method is general and can be extended to other diagnosis tasks of different cancer types based on WSIs.
summary: The whole slide histopathology images (WSIs) play a critical role in gastric cancer diagnosis. However, due to the large scale of WSIs and various sizes of the abnormal area, how to select informative regions and analyze them are quite challenging during the automatic diagnosis process. The multi-instance learning based on the most discriminative instances can be of great benefit for whole slide gastric image diagnosis. In this paper, we design a recalibrated multi-instance deep learning method (RMDL) to address this challenging problem. We first select the discriminative instances, and then utilize these instances to diagnose diseases based on the proposed RMDL approach. The designed RMDL network is capable of capturing instance-wise dependencies and recalibrating instance features according to the importance coefficient learned from the fused features. Furthermore, we build a large whole-slide gastric histopathology image dataset with detailed pixel-level annotations. Experimental results on the constructed gastric dataset demonstrate the significant improvement on the accuracy of our proposed framework compared with other state-of-the-art multi-instance learning methods. Moreover, our method is general and can be extended to other diagnosis tasks of different cancer types based on WSIs.
abstract_zh: 全玻片组织病理学图像（WSI）在胃癌诊断中发挥着至关重要的作用。然而，由于WSI规模庞大，异常区域大小各异，如何在自动诊断过程中选择信息丰富的区域并对其进行分析是相当具有挑战性的。基于最具辨别力的实例的多实例学习对于全幻灯片胃图像诊断有很大好处。在本文中，我们设计了一种重新校准的多实例深度学习方法（RMDL）来解决这一具有挑战性的问题。我们首先选择有判别性的实例，然后根据提出的 RMDL 方法利用这些实例来诊断疾病。设计的 RMDL 网络能够捕获实例依赖关系，并根据从融合特征中学习到的重要性系数重新校准实例特征。此外，我们建立了一个具有详细像素级注释的大型全幻灯片胃组织病理学图像数据集。构建的胃数据集的实验结果表明，与其他最先进的多实例学习方法相比，我们提出的框架的准确性有了显着提高。此外，我们的方法是通用的，可以扩展到基于 WSI 的不同癌症类型的其他诊断任务。

AI.motivation: 由于全切片组织病理学图像（WSIs）的规模较大且异常区域的大小不一，如何选择信息丰富的区域并进行分析在自动诊断过程中十分具有挑战性。基于最具有判别力的实例的多实例学习方法对全切片胃部图像诊断非常有益。
AI.method: 本文设计了一种重新校准的多实例深度学习方法（RMDL），首先选择具有判别力的实例，然后利用这些实例进行疾病诊断。RMDL网络能够捕捉实例间的依赖关系，并根据从融合特征中学习到的重要性系数重新校准实例特征。
AI.result: 在构建的胃部全切片组织病理学图像数据集上的实验结果表明，与其它最先进的多实例学习方法相比，本文提出的方法在准确率方面有显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 本文的方法具有通用性，可以扩展到基于WSIs的其他癌症类型的诊断任务中。

authors_text: Orith Toledo-Ronen,Matan Orbach,Yonatan Bilu,Artem Spector,Noam Slonim
title: Multilingual Argument Mining: Datasets and Analysis
title_zh: 多语言论据挖掘：数据集和分析
comment: Accepted to Findings of EMNLP 2020 (Long Paper). For the associated multilingual arguments and evidence corpus, see https://www.research.ibm.com/haifa/dept/vst/debating_data.shtml#Multilingual%20Argument%20Mining
comments: Accepted to Findings of EMNLP 2020 (Long Paper). For the associated multilingual arguments and evidence corpus, see https://www.research.ibm.com/haifa/dept/vst/debating_data.shtml#Multilingual%20Argument%20Mining
abstract: The growing interest in argument mining and computational argumentation brings with it a plethora of Natural Language Understanding (NLU) tasks and corresponding datasets. However, as with many other NLU tasks, the dominant language is English, with resources in other languages being few and far between. In this work, we explore the potential of transfer learning using the multilingual BERT model to address argument mining tasks in non-English languages, based on English datasets and the use of machine translation. We show that such methods are well suited for classifying the stance of arguments and detecting evidence, but less so for assessing the quality of arguments, presumably because quality is harder to preserve under translation. In addition, focusing on the translate-train approach, we show how the choice of languages for translation, and the relations among them, affect the accuracy of the resultant model. Finally, to facilitate evaluation of transfer learning on argument mining tasks, we provide a human-generated dataset with more than 10k arguments in multiple languages, as well as machine translation of the English datasets.
summary: The growing interest in argument mining and computational argumentation brings with it a plethora of Natural Language Understanding (NLU) tasks and corresponding datasets. However, as with many other NLU tasks, the dominant language is English, with resources in other languages being few and far between. In this work, we explore the potential of transfer learning using the multilingual BERT model to address argument mining tasks in non-English languages, based on English datasets and the use of machine translation. We show that such methods are well suited for classifying the stance of arguments and detecting evidence, but less so for assessing the quality of arguments, presumably because quality is harder to preserve under translation. In addition, focusing on the translate-train approach, we show how the choice of languages for translation, and the relations among them, affect the accuracy of the resultant model. Finally, to facilitate evaluation of transfer learning on argument mining tasks, we provide a human-generated dataset with more than 10k arguments in multiple languages, as well as machine translation of the English datasets.
abstract_zh: 人们对论证挖掘和计算论证的兴趣日益浓厚，随之而来的是大量自然语言理解 (NLU) 任务和相应的数据集。然而，与许多其他 NLU 任务一样，主要语言是英语，其他语言的资源很少。在这项工作中，我们基于英语数据集和机器翻译的使用，探索使用多语言 BERT 模型来解决非英语语言参数挖掘任务的迁移学习的潜力。我们表明，此类方法非常适合对论点立场进行分类和检测证据，但不太适合评估论点的质量，大概是因为翻译时质量更难保持。此外，我们重点关注翻译训练方法，展示了翻译语言的选择以及它们之间的关系如何影响最终模型的准确性。最后，为了促进参数挖掘任务上的迁移学习的评估，我们提供了一个包含超过 10k 个多种语言参数的人工生成数据集，以及英语数据集的机器翻译。

AI.tldr: 本文研究如何利用多语言BERT模型和迁移学习方法，在非英语语言中进行论证挖掘任务，并构建多语言数据集以支持评估。
AI.motivation: 当前论证挖掘任务和数据集主要集中在英语，缺乏其他语言资源，限制了模型在多语言场景下的应用。
AI.method: 基于英语数据集和机器翻译，采用多语言BERT模型进行迁移学习，对比不同语言翻译策略对模型性能的影响，并构建多语言人工标注数据集。
AI.result: 迁移学习方法在论证立场分类和证据检测任务中表现良好，但在论证质量评估任务中效果较差；不同语言对翻译策略的准确性有显著影响。
AI.conclusion: 多语言迁移学习在部分论证挖掘任务中有效，但需注意翻译对质量评估的影响；提供的多语言数据集可促进相关研究的评估与改进。

search_text: Multilingual Argument Mining: Datasets and Analysis 多语言论据挖掘：数据集和分析 The growing interest in argument mining and computational argumentation brings with it a plethora of Natural Language Understanding (NLU) tasks and corresponding datasets. However, as with many other NLU tasks, the dominant language is English, with resources in other languages being few and far between. In this work, we explore the potential of transfer learning using the multilingual BERT model to address argument mining tasks in non-English languages, based on English datasets and the use of machine translation. We show that such methods are well suited for classifying the stance of arguments and detecting evidence, but less so for assessing the quality of arguments, presumably because quality is harder to preserve under translation. In addition, focusing on the translate-train approach, we show how the choice of languages for translation, and the relations among them, affect the accuracy of the resultant model. Finally, to facilitate evaluation of transfer learning on argument mining tasks, we provide a human-generated dataset with more than 10k arguments in multiple languages, as well as machine translation of the English datasets. 人们对论证挖掘和计算论证的兴趣日益浓厚，随之而来的是大量自然语言理解 (NLU) 任务和相应的数据集。然而，与许多其他 NLU 任务一样，主要语言是英语，其他语言的资源很少。在这项工作中，我们基于英语数据集和机器翻译的使用，探索使用多语言 BERT 模型来解决非英语语言参数挖掘任务的迁移学习的潜力。我们表明，此类方法非常适合对论点立场进行分类和检测证据，但不太适合评估论点的质量，大概是因为翻译时质量更难保持。此外，我们重点关注翻译训练方法，展示了翻译语言的选择以及它们之间的关系如何影响最终模型的准确性。最后，为了促进参数挖掘任务上的迁移学习的评估，我们提供了一个包含超过 10k 个多种语言参数的人工生成数据集，以及英语数据集的机器翻译。 本文研究如何利用多语言BERT模型和迁移学习方法，在非英语语言中进行论证挖掘任务，并构建多语言数据集以支持评估。 Orith Toledo-Ronen,Matan Orbach,Yonatan Bilu,Artem Spector,Noam Slonim cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Esther Rodrigo Bonet,Duc Minh Nguyen,Nikos Deligiannis
title: Temporal Collaborative Filtering with Graph Convolutional Neural Networks
title_zh: 使用图卷积神经网络进行时间协同过滤
abstract: Temporal collaborative filtering (TCF) methods aim at modelling non-static aspects behind recommender systems, such as the dynamics in users' preferences and social trends around items. State-of-the-art TCF methods employ recurrent neural networks (RNNs) to model such aspects. These methods deploy matrix-factorization-based (MF-based) approaches to learn the user and item representations. Recently, graph-neural-network-based (GNN-based) approaches have shown improved performance in providing accurate recommendations over traditional MF-based approaches in non-temporal CF settings. Motivated by this, we propose a novel TCF method that leverages GNNs to learn user and item representations, and RNNs to model their temporal dynamics. A challenge with this method lies in the increased data sparsity, which negatively impacts obtaining meaningful quality representations with GNNs. To overcome this challenge, we train a GNN model at each time step using a set of observed interactions accumulated time-wise. Comprehensive experiments on real-world data show the improved performance obtained by our method over several state-of-the-art temporal and non-temporal CF models.
summary: Temporal collaborative filtering (TCF) methods aim at modelling non-static aspects behind recommender systems, such as the dynamics in users' preferences and social trends around items. State-of-the-art TCF methods employ recurrent neural networks (RNNs) to model such aspects. These methods deploy matrix-factorization-based (MF-based) approaches to learn the user and item representations. Recently, graph-neural-network-based (GNN-based) approaches have shown improved performance in providing accurate recommendations over traditional MF-based approaches in non-temporal CF settings. Motivated by this, we propose a novel TCF method that leverages GNNs to learn user and item representations, and RNNs to model their temporal dynamics. A challenge with this method lies in the increased data sparsity, which negatively impacts obtaining meaningful quality representations with GNNs. To overcome this challenge, we train a GNN model at each time step using a set of observed interactions accumulated time-wise. Comprehensive experiments on real-world data show the improved performance obtained by our method over several state-of-the-art temporal and non-temporal CF models.
abstract_zh: 时间协同过滤（TCF）方法旨在对推荐系统背后的非静态方面进行建模，例如用户偏好的动态和围绕项目的社交趋势。最先进的 TCF 方法采用循环神经网络 (RNN) 来对这些方面进行建模。这些方法部署基于矩阵分解（基于 ​​MF）的方法来学习用户和项目表示。最近，基于图神经网络（GNN）的方法在非时间 CF 设置中比传统的基于 MF 的方法在提供准确推荐方面表现出更好的性能。受此启发，我们提出了一种新颖的 TCF 方法，该方法利用 GNN 来学习用户和项目表示，并利用 RNN 来建模其时间动态。这种方法的挑战在于数据稀疏性的增加，这会对使用 GNN 获得有意义的质量表示产生负面影响。为了克服这一挑战，我们在每个时间步使用一组观察到的按时间累积的交互来训练 GNN 模型。对真实世界数据的综合实验表明，我们的方法比几种最先进的时态和非时态 CF 模型获得了更高的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合图卷积神经网络（GNN）和循环神经网络（RNN）的新型时间协同过滤方法，以解决推荐系统中用户偏好和社会趋势动态变化的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统时间协同过滤方法依赖RNN和矩阵分解（MF），但GNN在非时间协同过滤中表现更优。为提升推荐准确性，需结合GNN与RNN的优势，并解决数据稀疏性带来的表示学习难题。
AI.method: 利用GNN学习用户和物品的表示，通过RNN建模其时间动态；在每个时间步训练GNN模型，以缓解数据稀疏性对表示质量的影响。
AI.result: 在真实世界数据集上的实验表明，该方法在推荐性能上优于多个先进的时间和非时间协同过滤模型。
AI.conclusion: 所提出的方法有效结合了GNN和RNN的优势，克服了数据稀疏性挑战，在时间协同过滤任务中实现了更优的推荐效果。

search_text: Temporal Collaborative Filtering with Graph Convolutional Neural Networks 使用图卷积神经网络进行时间协同过滤 Temporal collaborative filtering (TCF) methods aim at modelling non-static aspects behind recommender systems, such as the dynamics in users' preferences and social trends around items. State-of-the-art TCF methods employ recurrent neural networks (RNNs) to model such aspects. These methods deploy matrix-factorization-based (MF-based) approaches to learn the user and item representations. Recently, graph-neural-network-based (GNN-based) approaches have shown improved performance in providing accurate recommendations over traditional MF-based approaches in non-temporal CF settings. Motivated by this, we propose a novel TCF method that leverages GNNs to learn user and item representations, and RNNs to model their temporal dynamics. A challenge with this method lies in the increased data sparsity, which negatively impacts obtaining meaningful quality representations with GNNs. To overcome this challenge, we train a GNN model at each time step using a set of observed interactions accumulated time-wise. Comprehensive experiments on real-world data show the improved performance obtained by our method over several state-of-the-art temporal and non-temporal CF models. 时间协同过滤（TCF）方法旨在对推荐系统背后的非静态方面进行建模，例如用户偏好的动态和围绕项目的社交趋势。最先进的 TCF 方法采用循环神经网络 (RNN) 来对这些方面进行建模。这些方法部署基于矩阵分解（基于 ​​MF）的方法来学习用户和项目表示。最近，基于图神经网络（GNN）的方法在非时间 CF 设置中比传统的基于 MF 的方法在提供准确推荐方面表现出更好的性能。受此启发，我们提出了一种新颖的 TCF 方法，该方法利用 GNN 来学习用户和项目表示，并利用 RNN 来建模其时间动态。这种方法的挑战在于数据稀疏性的增加，这会对使用 GNN 获得有意义的质量表示产生负面影响。为了克服这一挑战，我们在每个时间步使用一组观察到的按时间累积的交互来训练 GNN 模型。对真实世界数据的综合实验表明，我们的方法比几种最先进的时态和非时态 CF 模型获得了更高的性能。 本文提出一种结合图卷积神经网络（GNN）和循环神经网络（RNN）的新型时间协同过滤方法，以解决推荐系统中用户偏好和社会趋势动态变化的问题。 Esther Rodrigo Bonet,Duc Minh Nguyen,Nikos Deligiannis cs.AI

authors_text: Francesca Ronchini,Daniel Arteaga,Andrés Pérez-López
title: Sound event localization and detection based on crnn using rectangular filters and channel rotation data augmentation
title_zh: 使用矩形滤波器和通道旋转数据增强的基于 crnn 的声音事件定位和检测
comment: ef:Proceedings of the Detection and Classification of Acoustic Scenes and Events 2020 Workshop (DCASE2020)
comments: ef:Proceedings of the Detection and Classification of Acoustic Scenes and Events 2020 Workshop (DCASE2020)
abstract: Sound Event Localization and Detection refers to the problem of identifying the presence of independent or temporally-overlapped sound sources, correctly identifying to which sound class it belongs, estimating their spatial directions while they are active. In the last years, neural networks have become the prevailing method for sound Event Localization and Detection task, with convolutional recurrent neural networks being among the most used systems. This paper presents a system submitted to the Detection and Classification of Acoustic Scenes and Events 2020 Challenge Task 3. The algorithm consists of a convolutional recurrent neural network using rectangular filters, specialized in recognizing significant spectral features related to the task. In order to further improve the score and to generalize the system performance to unseen data, the training dataset size has been increased using data augmentation. The technique used for that is based on channel rotations and reflection on the xy plane in the First Order Ambisonic domain, which allows improving Direction of Arrival labels keeping the physical relationships between channels. Evaluation results on the development dataset show that the proposed system outperforms the baseline results, considerably improving Error Rate and F-score for location-aware detection.
summary: Sound Event Localization and Detection refers to the problem of identifying the presence of independent or temporally-overlapped sound sources, correctly identifying to which sound class it belongs, estimating their spatial directions while they are active. In the last years, neural networks have become the prevailing method for sound Event Localization and Detection task, with convolutional recurrent neural networks being among the most used systems. This paper presents a system submitted to the Detection and Classification of Acoustic Scenes and Events 2020 Challenge Task 3. The algorithm consists of a convolutional recurrent neural network using rectangular filters, specialized in recognizing significant spectral features related to the task. In order to further improve the score and to generalize the system performance to unseen data, the training dataset size has been increased using data augmentation. The technique used for that is based on channel rotations and reflection on the xy plane in the First Order Ambisonic domain, which allows improving Direction of Arrival labels keeping the physical relationships between channels. Evaluation results on the development dataset show that the proposed system outperforms the baseline results, considerably improving Error Rate and F-score for location-aware detection.
abstract_zh: 声音事件定位和检测是指识别独立或时间重叠的声源的存在、正确识别其所属的声音类别、估计它们活动时的空间方向的问题。在过去的几年中，神经网络已成为声音事件定位和检测任务的流行方法，其中卷积循环神经网络是最常用的系统之一。本文提出了一个提交给 2020 年声学场景和事件检测和分类挑战任务 3 的系统。该算法由使用矩形滤波器的卷积循环神经网络组成，专门用于识别与任务相关的重要频谱特征。为了进一步提高分数并将系统性能推广到未见过的数据，使用数据增强增加了训练数据集的大小。用于该目的的技术基于一阶高保真度立体声响复制域中 xy 平面上的通道旋转和反射，这允许改进到达方向标签，保持通道之间的物理关系。开发数据集的评估结果表明，所提出的系统优于基线结果，显着提高了位置感知检测的错误率和 F 分数。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于CRNN的声事件定位与检测系统，结合矩形滤波器和通道旋转数据增强技术，提升定位检测性能。
AI.motivation: 传统方法在处理重叠声源及空间方向估计存在挑战，需改进神经网络模型以提升SELD任务的准确率与泛化能力。
AI.method: 采用CRNN架构搭配矩形滤波器提取频谱特征，并通过Ambisonic域的通道旋转/反射进行数据增强，保持通道间物理关系。
AI.result: 开发集评估显示系统优于基线，显著降低定位错误率并提升F-score指标。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过数据增强与网络结构优化有效提升SELD性能，验证了其在实际场景中的可行性。

authors_text: Yuzhen Chen,Menghan Hu,Chunjun Hua,Guangtao Zhai,Jian Zhang,Qingli Li,Simon X. Yang
title: Face Mask Assistant: Detection of Face Mask Service Stage Based on Mobile Phone
title_zh: 口罩助手：基于手机检测口罩使用阶段
comment: 11 pages, 9 figures
comments: 11 pages, 9 figures
abstract: Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) has spread all over the world since it broke out massively in December 2019, which has caused a large loss to the whole world. Both the confirmed cases and death cases have reached a relatively frightening number. Syndrome coronaviruses 2 (SARS-CoV-2), the cause of COVID-19, can be transmitted by small respiratory droplets. To curb its spread at the source, wearing masks is a convenient and effective measure. In most cases, people use face masks in a high-frequent but short-time way. Aimed at solving the problem that we don't know which service stage of the mask belongs to, we propose a detection system based on the mobile phone. We first extract four features from the GLCMs of the face mask's micro-photos. Next, a three-result detection system is accomplished by using KNN algorithm. The results of validation experiments show that our system can reach a precision of 82.87% (standard deviation=8.5%) on the testing dataset. In future work, we plan to expand the detection objects to more mask types. This work demonstrates that the proposed mobile microscope system can be used as an assistant for face mask being used, which may play a positive role in fighting against COVID-19.
summary: Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) has spread all over the world since it broke out massively in December 2019, which has caused a large loss to the whole world. Both the confirmed cases and death cases have reached a relatively frightening number. Syndrome coronaviruses 2 (SARS-CoV-2), the cause of COVID-19, can be transmitted by small respiratory droplets. To curb its spread at the source, wearing masks is a convenient and effective measure. In most cases, people use face masks in a high-frequent but short-time way. Aimed at solving the problem that we don't know which service stage of the mask belongs to, we propose a detection system based on the mobile phone. We first extract four features from the GLCMs of the face mask's micro-photos. Next, a three-result detection system is accomplished by using KNN algorithm. The results of validation experiments show that our system can reach a precision of 82.87% (standard deviation=8.5%) on the testing dataset. In future work, we plan to expand the detection objects to more mask types. This work demonstrates that the proposed mobile microscope system can be used as an assistant for face mask being used, which may play a positive role in fighting against COVID-19.
abstract_zh: 2019冠状病毒病（COVID-19）自2019年12月大规模爆发以来，已在全球蔓延，给全世界造成了巨大损失。无论是确诊病例还是死亡病例，都达到了一个比较可怕的数字。综合症冠状病毒 2 (SARS-CoV-2) 是导致 COVID-19 的原因，可通过细小的呼吸道飞沫传播。从源头遏制疫情蔓延，佩戴口罩是便捷有效的措施。在大多数情况下，人们使用口罩的频率较高，但时间较短。针对我们不知道口罩属于哪个使用阶段的问题，我们提出了一种基于手机的检测系统。我们首先从口罩微照片的 GLCM 中提取四个特征。接下来，利用KNN算法完成了三结果检测系统。验证实验结果表明，我们的系统在测试数据集上可以达到82.87%（标准差=8.5%）的精度。在未来的工作中，我们计划将检测对象扩展到更多掩模类型。这项工作表明，所提出的移动显微镜系统可以作为使用口罩的助手，这可能在对抗COVID-19方面发挥积极作用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于手机的系统，用于检测口罩的使用阶段，以帮助用户判断口罩是否需要更换。
AI.motivation: 新冠疫情的全球爆发使口罩成为关键防护工具，但人们难以判断口罩的使用阶段，可能导致防护失效。本文旨在解决这一问题，提供一种便捷的检测方法。
AI.method: 从口罩微观照片的GLCM（灰度共生矩阵）中提取四个特征，结合KNN算法构建三分类检测系统。
AI.result: 在测试数据集上达到82.87%的检测精度（标准差8.5%）。
AI.conclusion: 该手机显微系统可作为口罩使用辅助工具，未来计划扩展至更多口罩类型以提升实用性。

ai_content.result: 在测试数据集上达到82.87%的检测精度（标准差8.5%）。
ai_content.conclusion: 该手机显微系统可作为口罩使用辅助工具，未来计划扩展至更多口罩类型以提升实用性。
